info.title: NMF AnyText Code generator
info.description: This package contains the code generator of the .NET Modelling Framework (NMF) AnyText packaged as a .NET Core Tool.

commands.name: generate-metamodel
commands.description: Generates a metamodel from an AnyText specification

options.name: --identifierNames
options.description: A list of identifier names, multiple entries separated by ';'. If omitted, attributes named 'name' or 'id' (case-insensitive) will be assumed to be identifiers.

options.name: --global-identifiers
options.description: If set, AnyText uses global identifiers instead of local identifiers

arguments.name: ANYTEXTPATH
arguments.description: The path to the AnyText specification

arguments.name: OUTPUTPATH
arguments.description: The output path, defaults to the same name as the AnyText specification but with changed extension.

commands.name: generate-parser
commands.description: Generates a parser for an AnyText specification

options.name: --namespace
options.description: The namespace of the generated parser class

options.name: --identifierNames
options.description: A list of identifier names, multiple entries separated by ';'. If omitted, attributes named 'name' or 'id' (case-insensitive) will be assumed to be identifiers.

arguments.name: ANYTEXTPATH
arguments.description: The path to the AnyText specification

arguments.name: OUTPUTPATH
arguments.description: The output path, defaults to the same name as the AnyText specification but with changed extension.

commands.name: generate-code
commands.description: Generates metamodel code from an AnyText specification or NMeta metamodel, specified in XMI or AnyMeta

options.name: --namespace
options.description: The namespace of the generated parser class

options.name: --save-metamodel-xmi
options.description: If set, the metamodel is kept in XMI format. This flag is ignored if the input is specified as XMI file.

options.name: --identifierNames
options.description: A list of identifier names, multiple entries separated by ';'. If omitted, attributes named 'name' or 'id' (case-insensitive) will be assumed to be identifiers. Ignored if the source is not an AnyText file.

options.name: --global-identifiers
options.description: If set, AnyText uses global identifiers instead of local identifiers

arguments.name: ANYTEXTPATH
arguments.description: The path to the AnyText specification
